title: Transport selection, dispatch, and HTTP fallback
purpose: Wire the WS transport into OpenAiProvider's Provider::stream() implementation. This is the final integration brief that connects the pool (PW-005), framing (PW-007), lifecycle (PW-008), and HTTP transport (PW-001) into a unified dispatch. WS is attempted first for known endpoints; HTTP is the fallback for custom endpoints and when WS is unavailable.
task: Modify OpenAiProvider to hold a WsPool. Update Provider::stream() to first try acquiring a WS connection from the pool. If successful, use the WS framing (PW-007) to send the request and stream events. If the pool returns a fallback signal (pool full, cooldown, custom endpoint), dispatch to the HTTP transport (PW-001). Log transport selection and fallback events.

requirements.title: WsPool on OpenAiProvider
requirements.spec: OpenAiProvider SHALL hold an Option<WsPool> initialized for known OpenAI endpoints (api.openai.com, chatgpt.com) and None for custom base_url endpoints.
acceptance: WsPool created during OpenAiProvider construction for known endpoints
Option<WsPool> is None for custom base_url
Pool config (min/max connections, idle timeout) sourced from ProviderConfig

requirements.title: WS-first dispatch in Provider::stream()
requirements.spec: WHEN Provider::stream() is called AND the provider has a WsPool THE SYSTEM SHALL first attempt to acquire a connection from the pool. If a connection is acquired, the spawned task SHALL use WS framing to send the request and stream events.
acceptance: stream() tries WS pool before HTTP for known endpoints
WS path uses PW-007 send/receive functions
Spawned task structure matches the existing HTTP pattern (mpsc channel + ReceiverStream)

requirements.title: HTTP fallback on pool unavailability
requirements.spec: WHEN the WS pool signals fallback (pool full, cooldown, establishment failure) OR when the provider has no WsPool (custom endpoint) THE SYSTEM SHALL dispatch to the HTTP transport (http.rs SenderProvider).
acceptance: Custom base_url (no WsPool) always uses HTTP
HTTP path is the exact extracted code from PW-001 — no reimplementation

requirements.title: Fallback logging
requirements.spec: WHEN the transport falls back from WS to HTTP THE SYSTEM SHALL log the event at warn level with the reason (pool full, cooldown, establishment failure, custom endpoint).
acceptance: tracing::warn emitted on every WS-to-HTTP fallback
Log message includes the specific reason for fallback
No warn log when HTTP is the expected transport (custom endpoint)

requirements.title: Pool state change logging
requirements.spec: Pool state changes (scale up, scale down, cooldown enter/exit) SHALL be logged at debug level.
acceptance: tracing::debug on pool scale-up (new slot created)
tracing::debug on pool scale-down (idle slot removed)
tracing::debug on cooldown enter and exit
tracing::debug on connection lifecycle events (establish, reconnect, close)

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the Provider trait — stream() signature stays the same
SHALL NOT reimplement the HTTP transport — use PW-001's http.rs directly
SHALL NOT hardcode endpoint detection patterns — use the existing base_url() method and constants
SHALL NOT add config values without discussion — pool config comes from ProviderConfig
verification: Integration: known endpoint uses WS pool, custom endpoint uses HTTP
Integration: pool-full scenario falls back to HTTP with warn log
Integration: existing HTTP-only tests still pass (behavioral parity)
grep for tracing::warn confirms fallback logging on all fallback paths
grep for tracing::debug confirms pool state change logging
